## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

title:	Add wterm.dev as third web terminal emulator
state:	OPEN
author:	thieso2
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	coderabbitai (Commented)
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	83
url:	https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/pull/83
additions:	134
deletions:	4
auto-merge:	disabled
--
## Summary
- Adds [@wterm/dom](https://wterm.dev/) 0.1.9 (Zig/WASM terminal from Vercel Labs) as a third renderer alongside Ghostty Web and xterm.js
- New Stimulus controller `wterm_terminal_controller.js` bridges ttyd's WebSocket protocol to `WTerm` (mirrors the existing ghostty/xterm controllers)
- Pinned via importmap to the jsDelivr `+esm` bundle, which transitively loads `@wterm/core` with the WASM blob inlined as base64 — no extra server setup, no separate WASM fetch
- CSS copied locally (`app/assets/stylesheets/wterm.css`), loaded conditionally from `layouts/terminal.html.erb` when the emulator is `wterm`
- User model validation + settings dropdown + guide page updated

No DB migration needed — the `terminal_emulator` column already exists as a string with default `"xterm"`.

## Test plan
- [ ] Open Settings → Terminal, select "wterm", verify form persists the choice
- [ ] Open tmux terminal on a running sandbox, verify wterm renders and accepts input/output, resize fires ttyd resize frames
- [ ] Open shell terminal, verify the same
- [ ] Switch back to Ghostty Web / xterm.js and confirm both still work
- [ ] Title updates (`onTitle`) propagate to `document.title`
- [ ] On reconnect (kill ttyd / restart sandbox), the 2s reconnect loop recovers

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added wterm as a new web terminal emulator option. Users can now select from three available renderers (Ghostty Web, xterm.js, and wterm) in Settings → Terminal.

* **Documentation**
  * Updated setup guides to document the new wterm terminal renderer option with reference materials.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

<% if @user.tailscale_enabled? %> <%= form_with url: toggle_tailscale_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "flex items-center gap-3" do |f| %> <% end %> <% else %>

<%= link_to "Enable Tailscale", tailscale_path, class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-700 underline" %> to connect your sandboxes to your tailnet.

<% end %>
<%# ── VNC Section ── %>

VNC

<%= form_with model: @user, url: update_profile_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "flex items-center gap-3" do |f| %> <% end %>

When enabled, your Chrome extensions, bookmarks, and settings are saved in ~/.config/google-chrome and persist when you destroy and recreate sandboxes.

<%# ── Terminal Section ── %>

Terminal

<%= form_with model: @user, url: update_profile_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "flex items-center gap-3" do |f| %> <%= f.select :terminal_emulator, [["Ghostty Web", "ghostty"], ["xterm.js", "xterm"], ["wterm", "wterm"]], {}, onchange: "this.form.requestSubmit()", class: "rounded-md border-gray-300 text-sm focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500" %> <% end %>

Choose which terminal emulator to use in the browser. Ghostty Web uses Ghostty's WASM-based parser. xterm.js is the classic JavaScript terminal. wterm (wterm.dev) is a Zig/WASM terminal from Vercel Labs.

<%# ── Custom Links Section ── %>

Custom Links

Add custom links that appear on each sandbox card. Use template variables in the URL: {user}, {hostname}, {ssh_port}, {sandbox}, {tailscale_ip}, {tmux_cmd}, {tmux_cmd_encoded}

<%= form_with url: update_custom_links_settings_path, method: :patch do |f| %>
<% (@user.custom_links.presence || []).each_with_index do |link, i| %>
" placeholder="Name (e.g. LaTerminal)" class="w-full px-2 py-1.5 border border-gray-300 rounded text-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 mb-1"> " placeholder="URL template (e.g. ssh://{user}@{hostname}:{ssh_port})" class="w-full px-2 py-1.5 border border-gray-300 rounded text-sm font-mono focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500">
<% end %>
<% if @api_tokens.any? %>
<% @api_tokens.each do |token| %>

<%= token.name %>

<%= token.prefix %>

Created <%= time_ago_in_words(token.created_at) %> ago <% if token.last_used_at %> · Last used <%= time_ago_in_words(token.last_used_at) %> ago <% else %> · Never used <% end %>

<%= button_to "Revoke", revoke_token_settings_path(id: token.id), method: :delete, class: "ml-3 px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-red-700 text-xs whitespace-nowrap", data: { confirm_message: "Revoke '#{token.name}'? Applications using this token will stop working." } %>
<% end %>
<% else %>

No API tokens generated yet. Create one to use the Sandcastle CLI and API.

<% end %>

Tip: Use your API token with the Sandcastle CLI by running sandcastle token create

<%# ── Sandbox Defaults Section ── %>

Sandbox Defaults

Pre-checked options on the "Create Sandcastle" form. You can still override any of these when creating an individual sandbox.

<%= form_with model: @user, url: update_profile_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "space-y-4" do |f| %>

Start a virtual display (Xvnc) for GUI apps and browser access.

Persist ~/ across sandboxes.

Enables Docker-in-Docker inside each new sandbox.

When SSHing in, automatically attach to a shared tmux session so your work survives disconnects.

Mount home/persisted as a network drive via SMB3 over Tailscale. Requires Tailscale enabled and an SMB password set in the Network tab.

<%= f.label :default_data_path, "Default data path", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :default_data_path, placeholder: "e.g., . (all user data) or projects", class: "block w-full max-w-xs rounded-md border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 font-mono" %>

Mount this directory to /persisted in new sandboxes. Leave blank to disable.

<% end %>
<%# ── Terminal Section ── %>

Terminal

<%= form_with model: @user, url: update_profile_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "flex items-center gap-3" do |f| %> <%= f.select :terminal_emulator, [["Ghostty Web", "ghostty"], ["xterm.js", "xterm"]], {}, onchange: "this.form.requestSubmit()", class: "rounded-md border-gray-300 text-sm focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500" %> <% end %>

Choose which terminal emulator to use in the browser. Ghostty Web uses Ghostty's WASM-based parser. xterm.js is the classic JavaScript terminal.

<%# ── Sandbox Archive Section ── %>

Sandbox Archive

<% default_retention_days = Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30 %>

When you remove a sandbox it is archived (soft-deleted) instead of immediately purged, so you can restore it if needed. Leave blank to use the system default (<%= default_retention_days %> days). Set to 0 to disable archiving (immediate purge on remove).

<%= form_with model: @user, url: update_profile_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "flex items-end gap-3" do |f| %>
<%= f.label :sandbox_archive_retention_days, "Retention period (days)", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.number_field :sandbox_archive_retention_days, min: 0, max: 365, placeholder: "#{default_retention_days} (system default)", class: "w-40 px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500" %>
<% end %>

<%= form_with url: update_custom_links_settings_path, method: :patch do |f| %>
<% (@user.custom_links.presence || []).each_with_index do |link, i| %>

<% end %>
<% end %>

Example: laterminal://open/{user}@{hostname}:{ssh_port}?command={tmux_cmd_encoded} — open sandbox in LaTerminal with tmux

Example: ssh://{user}@{hostname}:{ssh_port} — open plain SSH connection

{tmux_cmd} expands to <%= User::TMUX_CMD %> (plain text). {tmux_cmd_encoded} is the URL-encoded version (for query parameters).

Show on: controls which devices display the link — all, desktop, tablet, or phone

<%# ── Tailscale Section ── %>

Tailscale

Status

<% case @user.tailscale_state %> <% when "enabled" %> Enabled <% when "pending" %> Pending authentication <% else %> Disabled <% end %>

<%= link_to "Enable Tailscale", tailscale_path, class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-700 underline" %> to connect your sandboxes to your tailnet.

<% end %>
<%# ── SMB / File Sharing Section ── %>

File Sharing (SMB)

Set an SMB password to mount your sandbox's home directory or /persisted as a network drive from macOS, Windows, or Linux. Enable SMB per-sandbox when creating or editing a sandbox. Requires Tailscale for access.

<%= form_with url: update_smb_password_settings_path, method: :patch, class: "space-y-4" do |f| %>
<%= password_field_tag "user[smb_password]", nil, placeholder: @user.smb_password.present? ? "••••••••" : "Set a password for SMB access", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500" %> <% if @user.smb_password.present? %>

SMB password is set. Enter a new value to change it.

<% else %>

No SMB password set. SMB will not start even if enabled on a sandbox.

<% end %>

macOS: smb://<tailscale-ip>/home

Windows: \\<tailscale-ip>\home

Linux: mount -t cifs //<tailscale-ip>/home /mnt -o username=<you>

<% end %>
<%# ══════ Files & Keys tab ══════ %>
<%# ── SSH Keys Section ── %>

SSH Keys

Add your SSH public keys to access sandboxes via SSH. Multiple keys are supported (e.g. one per device).

<%= form_with url: update_ssh_keys_settings_path, method: :patch do |f| %>
<% (@user.ssh_keys.presence || []).each_with_index do |entry, i| %>
" placeholder="Label (e.g. MacBook, iPad)" class="w-48 px-2 py-1.5 border border-gray-300 rounded text-sm focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500">
<% if entry["key"].present? %>

<%= entry["key"].split(" ").first %> ...<%= entry["key"].split(" ").last(1).first&.last(12) %>

<% end %>
<% end %>
<% end %>
<%# ── Persisted Directories Section ── %>

Persisted Directories

Per-user directories under $HOME that survive sandbox destroy/recreate. Files written inside a sandbox round-trip back to the host. Use this for OAuth credentials that the CLI rewrites (e.g. .claude, .codex).

<%= form_with url: update_persisted_paths_settings_path, method: :patch do |f| %>
<% (@user.persisted_paths.order(:path)).each do |pp| %>
<% end %>
<% end %>
<%# ── Injected Files Section ── %>

Injected Files

Files dropped into $HOME on every new sandbox. Stored encrypted; written via docker exec after start (never appears in container env or docker inspect). Use for static credentials and dotfiles (.npmrc, .aws/credentials, .config/op/config).

<%= form_with url: update_injected_files_settings_path, method: :patch do |f| %>
<% @user.injected_files.order(:path).each do |inj| %>
" placeholder="600" title="Octal mode" class="w-20 px-2 py-1.5 border border-gray-300 rounded text-sm font-mono focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500">
<% if inj.content.present? %>

<%= inj.content.bytesize %> bytes stored · leave content blank to keep unchanged

<% end %>
<% end %>
<% end %>

content.content:
[tasks.dev]
description = "Create/migrate DB if needed, then start the dev server"
run = """
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
bin/rails db:prepare
bin/dev
"""

[tasks."deploy:dev"]
description = "Run local development with live source mounting"
run = """
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# External bridge network shared with sandbox/WeTTY/Tailscale containers
if ! docker network inspect sandcastle-web >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Creating sandcastle-web network..."
  docker network create sandcastle-web
fi

# Detect Docker socket GID so the web/worker containers can talk to dockerd
if [ -z "${DOCKER_GID:-}" ]; then
  if [ "$(uname)" = "Darwin" ]; then
    DOCKER_GID=$(stat -f '%g' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  else
    DOCKER_GID=$(stat -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  fi
  export DOCKER_GID
fi

# Default host for Traefik routing; override with DEV_HOST=foo.local
export DEV_HOST="${DEV_HOST:-localhost}"

# Pick a UID/GID for the in-container sandcastle user. Default to the host
# user's IDs so bind-mounted source files stay owned by you on disk. If the
# outer environment uses a restricted user namespace (e.g. Sysbox), the
# production default of 220568 would exceed the range and fail with EINVAL
# on chown/setgroups — the host UID is always within range.
if [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_UID:-}" ]; then
  if [ -r /proc/self/uid_map ]; then
    range=$(awk 'END{print $3}' /proc/self/uid_map)
    if [ -n "$range" ] && [ "$range" -lt 220569 ]; then
      echo "Detected restricted userns (range=$range); using host UID/GID for sandcastle user."
      SANDCASTLE_UID=$(id -u)
      SANDCASTLE_GID=$(id -g)
    fi
  fi
fi
export SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
export SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"

echo "Starting development environment with live reload..."

[tasks."release"]
alias = "release:patch"
description = "Bump patch version, tag, push, and wait for CI to build the release"
run = """
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Check if HEAD already has a tag
if git describe --exact-match --tags HEAD 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: Current commit already has a tag. Make new commits before releasing."
  exit 1
fi

# Get the latest tag
latest=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "v0.0.0")
echo "Current version: $latest"

# Parse and bump patch
IFS='.' read -r major minor patch <<< "${latest#v}"
new_version="v${major}.${minor}.$((patch + 1))"
echo "New version:     $new_version"

# Tag and push
git tag "$new_version"
git push origin "$new_version"
echo ""
echo "Tag $new_version pushed. GitHub Actions will build the release."
echo "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/actions"
"""

  exit 1
fi

latest=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "v0.0.0")
echo "Current version: $latest"

IFS='.' read -r major minor patch <<< "${latest#v}"
new_version="v${major}.$((minor + 1)).0"
echo "New version:     $new_version"

git tag "$new_version"
git push origin "$new_version"
echo ""

"""

# Check if HEAD already has a tag

  echo "ERROR: Current commit already has a tag. Make new commits before releasing."
  exit 1
fi

latest=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "v0.0.0")

IFS='.' read -r major minor patch <<< "${latest#v}"

# sandcastle-sandbox-base — heavy dependencies that rarely change.
# Published as ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox-base:latest
# Rebuild only when system packages, Docker, Chrome, or dev tools change.

# Build websockify-go: minimal WebSocket→TCP proxy (~5 MB static binary)
FROM golang:1.26-alpine AS websockify-builder
WORKDIR /build
COPY websockify/ .
# go mod tidy downloads deps and generates go.sum; -mod=mod allows updating go.sum at build time
RUN go mod tidy && CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o websockify .

FROM ubuntu:25.10

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    openssh-server sudo curl git tmux vim neovim \
    build-essential \
    jq ripgrep fd-find htop wget unzip ca-certificates net-tools iproute2 iputils-ping \
    mosh samba \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI (gh)
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
        -o /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
        > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y gh \

# Install mise + Node.js + Codex.
# mise data/config live in /usr/local/share/mise and /usr/local/etc/mise so
# the user can run `mise install` without sudo. entrypoint.sh chowns both
# trees to the sandbox user on startup (single-user per sandbox).
# Retry: mise install/exec can fail transiently in Docker builds (exit 127).
ENV MISE_DATA_DIR=/usr/local/share/mise
ENV MISE_CONFIG_DIR=/usr/local/etc/mise
RUN curl https://mise.run | MISE_INSTALL_PATH=/usr/local/bin/mise sh \
    && mkdir -p /usr/local/etc/mise \
    && /usr/local/bin/mise use --global node@lts \
    && /usr/local/bin/mise install \
    && for attempt in 1 2 3; do \
         /usr/local/bin/mise exec -- npm install -g @openai/codex \
         && break; \
         echo "Retry $attempt: mise exec failed, retrying in 5s..."; sleep 5; \
       done

# Claude Code — standalone native binary (no Node.js dependency).
# Installed off $PATH at /opt/claude/claude so entrypoint.sh can seed a
# per-user copy into ~/.local/bin/claude. That seed lives in the user's
# bind-mounted $HOME, so `claude --update` persists across sandbox
# recreations and survives base-image rebuilds.
RUN VERSION=$(curl -fsSL https://storage.googleapis.com/claude-code-dist-86c565f3-f756-42ad-8dfa-d59b1c096819/claude-code-releases/latest) \
    && ARCH=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "arm64" ]; then PLATFORM="linux-arm64"; else PLATFORM="linux-x64"; fi \
    && mkdir -p /opt/claude \
    && curl -fsSL "https://storage.googleapis.com/claude-code-dist-86c565f3-f756-42ad-8dfa-d59b1c096819/claude-code-releases/$VERSION/$PLATFORM/claude" \
       -o /opt/claude/claude \
    && chmod 0755 /opt/claude/claude

#!/bin/bash
set -e

USERNAME="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandbox}"
SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY:-}"

# Create user if not exists
if ! id "$USERNAME" &>/dev/null; then
    useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo,docker "$USERNAME"
    echo "$USERNAME ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
    chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
fi

# mise data/config dirs are shipped root-owned in the base image. Since each
# sandbox is for one user, hand ownership over so `mise install/use` works
# without sudo (otherwise runtime installs fail with EACCES on create_dir_all).
for d in /usr/local/share/mise /usr/local/etc/mise; do
    [ -d "$d" ] && chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "$d" 2>/dev/null || true
done

# Set up SSH authorized keys (append if not already present, preserving
# any WeTTY keys that may have been injected for other sandboxes sharing
# this user's home directory via bind mount).
# SSH_KEY may contain multiple keys separated by newlines.
if [ -n "$SSH_KEY" ]; then
    SSH_DIR="/home/$USERNAME/.ssh"
    mkdir -p "$SSH_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
    if [ -d "$SSH_DIR" ]; then
        touch "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
        while IFS= read -r key; do

# Thin layer on top of the pre-built base image.
# Changes here (entrypoint, config, scripts) build in seconds.
# To rebuild the base: trigger the "Rebuild Sandbox Base Image" workflow
# or change files in images/sandbox/base/.
FROM ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox-base:latest

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

# Always pull latest security patches and package updates.
# CACHE_DATE is set at build time to bust the layer cache on demand.
#
# Hold Docker-stack packages before upgrading: the base image pins
# containerd.io=1.7.27-1 (see Dockerfile.base for rationale — containerd.io ≥ 2.x
# breaks sysbox-runc, nestybox/sysbox#958). Without the hold, `apt-get upgrade`
# installs containerd.io 2.x, which also replaces /usr/bin/runc with a newer
# runc (1.3.3+) whose CVE-2025-52881 fix rejects sysbox's FUSE-backed procfs
# with "unsafe procfs detected" and breaks docker-in-docker inside sandboxes.
# Re-install the pinned runc afterwards as belt-and-suspenders in case an
# older base image (built before the apt-mark hold was added) is used.
ARG CACHE_DATE=0
RUN apt-mark hold docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin \
    && for i in 1 2 3; do apt-get update && break || sleep 5; done \
    && apt-get upgrade -y \
    && RUNC_VERSION="v1.1.15" \
    && ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture) \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/download/${RUNC_VERSION}/runc.${ARCH}" \
       -o /usr/bin/runc \
    && chmod +x /usr/bin/runc \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

Test passes — `mise use --global python@3.13` succeeds as UID 1001 with the build-time chown, and writes to `/usr/local/etc/mise/config.toml`. The change is verified.

Summary:
- `chown -R 1001:1001 /usr/local/share/mise /usr/local/etc/mise` at build time works as intended
- Default user is reliably 1001 (`ubuntu` already at 1000 in `ubuntu:25.10`)
- Entrypoint chown loop removed; `mise install/use` runs without sudo for the sandbox user

fix(sandbox): chown mise dirs to UID 1001 at image build time

Move the mise dir chown from entrypoint.sh into the Dockerfile.base RUN
that installs mise. The base image's `ubuntu` user occupies UID 1000, so
the runtime `useradd` always assigns UID 1001 — chown to that numeric UID
at build time so the work isn't repeated on every container start.

EOF
)" && git push origin main
